facts.text: Chess originated from an ancient Indian game called Chaturanga, which dates back to the 6th century AD.
The Sicilian Defense (1.e4 c5) is the most popular response to 1.e4 at the highest levels of chess.
Bobby Fischer became the 11th World Chess Champion by defeating Boris Spassky in Reykjavik, Iceland.
Magnus Carlsen achieved the highest ELO rating in history, reaching 2882 in May 2014.
A fork is a tactic where a single piece attacks two or more enemy pieces simultaneously.
Chess.com surpassed 100 million members, making it the largest online chess platform in the world.
The Queen's Gambit on Netflix became the most-watched scripted limited series on the platform in 2020.
Deep Blue defeated Garry Kasparov in a six-game match in 1997, the first time a computer beat a reigning world champion under standard time controls.
Judit Polgár is widely regarded as the strongest female chess player in history, reaching a peak rating of 2735.
The Immortal Game was played between Adolf Anderssen and Lionel Kieseritzky in London in 1851.
Castling is the only move in chess where two pieces move at the same time — the king and a rook.
Garry Kasparov became the youngest undisputed World Chess Champion at age 22 in 1985.
The Ruy López opening, named after a 16th-century Spanish priest, is one of the oldest recorded openings in chess.
Hikaru Nakamura became one of the most popular chess streamers on Twitch, regularly drawing tens of thousands of viewers.
AlphaZero, developed by DeepMind, taught itself chess in just four hours and defeated Stockfish in a 100-game match in 2017.
The word 'checkmate' comes from the Persian phrase 'shāh māt,' meaning 'the king is helpless.'
En passant is a special pawn capture that can only occur immediately after a pawn moves two squares forward from its starting position.
Viswanathan Anand became the first Indian to win the World Chess Championship, earning the title in 2000.
The Game of the Century was played by 13-year-old Bobby Fischer against Donald Byrne in New York in 1956.
Lichess is a free, open-source chess server founded by Thibault Duplessis in 2010.
A pin is a tactic where an attacked piece cannot move without exposing a more valuable piece behind it.
The Polgár sisters — Susan, Sofia, and Judit — were part of an experiment by their father László to prove that geniuses are made, not born.
Chess spread from India to Persia, where it was known as Shatranj, and then throughout the Arab world.
The Italian Game (1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.Bc4) is one of the oldest recorded chess openings, dating back to the 15th century.
Stockfish is one of the strongest open-source chess engines and has won multiple Top Chess Engine Championships.
Bobby Fischer was born in Chicago, Illinois.
A skewer is the reverse of a pin — the more valuable piece is attacked first and must move, exposing a less valuable piece behind it.
Ding Liren became China's first World Chess Champion by defeating Ian Nepomniachtchi in 2023.
The ELO rating system, designed by Arpad Elo, was adopted by FIDE in 1970 to rank chess players worldwide.
The Queen's Gambit (1.d4 d5 2.c4) is one of the oldest known chess openings, first mentioned in a 15th-century manuscript.
PogChamps was a chess tournament featuring popular Twitch streamers that helped bring chess to a wider online audience.
Anatoly Karpov held the World Chess Championship title from 1975 to 1985, defending it successfully multiple times.
The Opera Game, played by Paul Morphy in 1858 at an opera house in Paris, is considered one of the most elegant games ever played.
Chess clocks were first used in competitive play in 1883 during the London tournament.
A discovered attack occurs when one piece moves out of the way, revealing an attack from another piece behind it.
The French Defense (1.e4 e6) was named after a correspondence match between London and Paris in 1834.
Magnus Carlsen was born in Tønsberg, Norway.
Chess notation allows players to record every move of a game using a system of letters and numbers.
The King's Indian Defense is a hypermodern opening where Black allows White to build a strong pawn center before counterattacking.
Kasparov and Karpov's 1984-85 World Championship match was the longest in history, lasting 48 games before being controversially terminated.
Blitz chess is a fast-paced format where each player typically has 3 to 5 minutes for the entire game.
José Raúl Capablanca, the Cuban World Champion, was known for his extraordinary endgame technique and intuitive style.
Emanuel Lasker held the World Chess Championship title for 27 years (1894–1921), the longest reign in history.
The Caro-Kann Defense (1.e4 c6) is known for producing solid but slightly passive positions for Black.
Chess in Central Park, New York City, is a beloved tradition where players of all skill levels gather to play.
Leela Chess Zero (Lc0) is a neural network-based chess engine inspired by DeepMind's AlphaZero.
Mikhail Tal, known as 'The Magician from Riga,' was famous for his brilliant sacrificial attacking style.
The stalemate rule — where a player with no legal moves but not in check draws the game — has been debated for centuries.
Sergey Karjakin became the youngest grandmaster in history at age 12 years and 7 months in 2002.
The English Opening (1.c4) is named after the English master Howard Staunton, who favored it in the 19th century.
Fischer Random Chess (Chess960) randomizes the starting positions of pieces on the back rank to reduce reliance on opening theory.
Garry Kasparov was born in Baku, Azerbaijan.
Pawn structure is a key strategic concept — pawns cannot move backward and often determine the character of a position.
The Netflix series 'The Queen's Gambit' was based on Walter Tevis's 1983 novel of the same name.
In bullet chess, each player has only 1 minute for the entire game, requiring extremely fast decision-making.
Viswanathan Anand was born in Chennai, India.
The Evergreen Game, played by Adolf Anderssen against Jean Dufresne in 1852, features a spectacular queen sacrifice.
Bughouse is a popular chess variant played by teams of two, where captured pieces are passed to a partner.
The bishop was originally an elephant piece in Chaturanga before its movement changed as the game evolved in Europe.
Alexander Alekhine was the only World Chess Champion to die while still holding the title, in 1946.
The Sicilian Najdorf variation is named after Argentine grandmaster Miguel Najdorf and is one of the sharpest openings in chess.
Beth Harmon, the fictional protagonist of The Queen's Gambit, was inspired by several real-life chess prodigies.
Chess was included as a mind sport at the 2006 Asian Games in Doha, Qatar.
The longest possible chess game in terms of moves is 5,949 moves, based on the 50-move and 75-move rules.
Bobby Fischer demanded and received unprecedented conditions for his 1972 World Championship match against Spassky.
The Grünfeld Defense (1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 g6 3.Nc3 d5) was a favorite weapon of Garry Kasparov.
The first official World Chess Championship match was held in 1886 between Wilhelm Steinitz and Johannes Zukertort.
A back-rank mate occurs when the king is trapped behind its own pawns and checkmated by a rook or queen on the last rank.
The COVID-19 pandemic combined with The Queen's Gambit series caused an unprecedented boom in chess popularity in 2020.
Anatoly Karpov was born in Zlatoust, Soviet Union.
The Nimzo-Indian Defense (1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 e6 3.Nc3 Bb4) is one of the most respected defenses against 1.d4.
The 50-move rule states that a player can claim a draw if no capture or pawn move has been made in the last 50 moves.
Speed chess, including blitz and rapid formats, has become increasingly popular in online and over-the-board tournaments.
In 1997, IBM's Deep Blue used specialized hardware capable of evaluating 200 million positions per second.
The Scotch Game (1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.d4) was popularized by Garry Kasparov in his World Championship matches.
Chess is played in over 150 countries and is governed internationally by FIDE (Fédération Internationale des Échecs).
Mikhail Botvinnik, the 'Patriarch of Soviet Chess,' held the World Championship three separate times between 1948 and 1963.
A zugzwang is a position where any move a player makes worsens their position, but they are compelled to move.
Bobby Fischer's 1972 victory over Spassky was seen as a Cold War battle between the United States and the Soviet Union.
The London System (1.d4 followed by Bf4) has become extremely popular at the amateur level due to its simplicity.
Judit Polgár defeated Garry Kasparov in a rapid game in 2002, one of the most significant moments in chess history.
The pawn is the only chess piece that captures differently from how it moves — it moves forward but captures diagonally.
Ding Liren was born in Wenzhou, China.
The Marshall Attack in the Ruy López involves a dramatic pawn sacrifice for long-term attacking chances.
FIDE (World Chess Federation) was founded in Paris, France.
The concept of 'tempo' in chess refers to the value of a single move — gaining or losing a tempo can be critical.
Magnus Carlsen won his first World Chess Championship by defeating Viswanathan Anand in Chennai in 2013.
The Philidor Defense (1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 d6) is named after the 18th-century French player François-André Danican Philidor.
A chess grandmaster title, once earned, is held for life — it can never be taken away by FIDE.
Alekhine's Defense (1.e4 Nf6) is a provocative opening where Black immediately attacks White's e4 pawn.
The world record for the most simultaneous chess games was set by Ehsan Ghaem-Maghami, who played 604 games at once in 2011.
Kasparov and Karpov played five World Championship matches between 1984 and 1990, one of the greatest rivalries in sports history.
The Benoni Defense is an aggressive opening for Black that aims for dynamic counterplay against White's pawn center.
International Master (IM) and Grandmaster (GM) are the two highest titles awarded by FIDE to chess players.
The queen became the most powerful piece on the board during the late 15th century rules reform in Europe.
Passed pawns — pawns with no opposing pawns blocking or guarding their advance — are a major asset in endgames.
José Raúl Capablanca was born in Havana, Cuba.
The Dragon variation of the Sicilian Defense is named for the pawn formation that resembles the constellation Draco.
Chess puzzles, also called chess problems, are positions where the solver must find the best move or sequence of moves.
AlphaZero's playing style was described as creative and unconventional, often making moves that surprised human grandmasters.
The Dutch Defense (1.d4 f5) is an aggressive opening for Black that immediately fights for control of the e4 square.
Wilhelm Steinitz is considered the first official World Chess Champion, holding the title from 1886 to 1894.
The opposition is a key endgame concept in king-and-pawn endings where the player not on the move has the advantage.
Emanuel Lasker was born in Berlinchen, Prussia (now Barlinek, Poland).
Chess engines have become so strong that even top grandmasters cannot compete with them in standard games.
The Sveshnikov variation of the Sicilian Defense features an early ...e5 push and has been used by many top players.
Tigran Petrosian was known as 'Iron Tigran' for his nearly impenetrable defensive style of play.
A sacrifice in chess is when a player deliberately gives up material for positional or tactical compensation.
The Candidates Tournament determines which player will challenge the reigning World Champion for the title.
Bobby Fischer created Fischer Random Chess (Chess960) in 1996 to combat the growing influence of opening preparation.
The Scandinavian Defense (1.e4 d5) is one of the oldest recorded openings and immediately challenges White's e4 pawn.
Mikhail Tal was born in Riga, Latvia.
An isolated queen's pawn (IQP) is a common pawn structure that provides dynamic chances but can become a weakness in endgames.
The annual Tata Steel Chess Tournament in Wijk aan Zee, Netherlands, is one of the most prestigious events in chess.
Garry Kasparov retired from professional chess in 2005 to focus on politics and writing.
The Bird's Opening (1.f4) is named after the 19th-century English player Henry Bird.
A fianchetto involves developing a bishop to g2 or b2 (for White) or g7 or b7 (for Black), placing it on a long diagonal.
The Sinquefield Cup in St. Louis, Missouri, regularly attracts the world's strongest players.
The Berlin Defense in the Ruy López became known as the 'Berlin Wall' for its solid, drawish reputation.
The first chess-playing automaton, 'The Turk,' was built in 1770 and was later revealed to be a hoax with a human inside.
Doubled pawns — two pawns of the same color on the same file — are generally considered a structural weakness.
Magnus Carlsen relinquished his World Championship title in 2022, choosing not to defend it against the next challenger.
The Petrov Defense (1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nf6) is a solid but somewhat drawish opening at the top level.
Susan Polgár (Zsuzsa) was the first woman to earn the full Grandmaster title through the traditional rating system.
Chess has been proposed multiple times for inclusion in the Olympic Games but has not yet been accepted.
A zwischenzug (intermediate move) is an unexpected move played before the expected response, often changing the evaluation.
The Slav Defense (1.d4 d5 2.c4 c6) is one of the most solid defenses against the Queen's Gambit.
Paul Morphy, an American chess prodigy from New Orleans, was considered the unofficial world champion in the 1850s.
The principle of two weaknesses states that to win a position, the attacker often needs to create pressure on two fronts simultaneously.
Hikaru Nakamura is one of the fastest blitz and bullet chess players in history, with numerous speed chess titles.
The Staunton chess set, designed in 1849, became the standard set used in all official chess competitions.
The concept of 'compensation' in chess refers to non-material advantages (like initiative or attack) that offset a material deficit.
Kasparov's match against Deep Blue in 1996 ended 4–2 in Kasparov's favor, but the rematch in 1997 went to the machine.
The Closed Sicilian (with 2.Nc3 and 3.g3) avoids the complex Open Sicilian lines and leads to a more positional game.
Chess prodigies often achieve grandmaster status in their early teens — Abhimanyu Mishra became the youngest GM ever at 12 years, 4 months.
The Lucena position is a fundamental rook endgame position that demonstrates how to promote a pawn with a rook.
Fischer's 6-0 whitewash of Mark Taimanov in the 1971 Candidates matches was one of the most dominant performances in chess history.
The concept of 'outpost' refers to a square where a piece (usually a knight) is firmly established and cannot be attacked by enemy pawns.
Correspondence chess, played by mail or email, allows players days or weeks to analyze each move.
The Giuoco Piano (Italian for 'quiet game') is a slower, more positional approach within the Italian Game.
Arpad Elo, the creator of the ELO rating system, was born in Hungary but spent most of his life in the United States.
A perpetual check occurs when one player can continuously check the opponent's king, resulting in a draw.
The World Rapid and Blitz Chess Championships are held annually and attract nearly all top grandmasters.
Kasparov's famous 'Immortal Game' against Veselin Topalov in 1999 featured a stunning series of sacrifices.
The Pirc Defense (1.e4 d6 2.d4 Nf6 3.Nc3 g6) is a hypermodern opening that allows White to build a strong center.
Pawn promotion occurs when a pawn reaches the eighth rank and can be converted into any piece except a king.
The Philidor position in rook endgames demonstrates a drawing technique where the defending side uses their rook on the third rank.
José Raúl Capablanca lost only 34 serious games in his entire career, an extraordinary achievement.
The St. Louis Chess Club has become the premier chess venue in the United States.
A battery in chess is formed when two or more pieces of the same type line up on a rank, file, or diagonal.
The Modern Defense (1.e4 g6) is a flexible opening that can transpose into many different structures.
FIDE awards the title of Woman Grandmaster (WGM) with lower requirements than the standard GM title.
The Evans Gambit (1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.Bc4 Bc5 4.b4) is an aggressive opening offering a pawn for rapid development.
Garry Kasparov's peak ELO rating of 2851 stood as the record for nearly 20 years until Magnus Carlsen surpassed it.
The exchange sacrifice — giving up a rook for a bishop or knight — is a common positional tool, especially in Sicilian structures.
The first international chess tournament was held in London in 1851, won by Adolf Anderssen.
The Semi-Slav Defense combines ideas from the Slav and Queen's Gambit Declined and leads to rich, complex positions.
Chess blindfold play involves playing without seeing the board, relying entirely on memory and visualization.
Howard Staunton was one of the strongest players in the world during the 1840s and the Staunton chess set bears his name.
The King's Gambit (1.e4 e5 2.f4) was one of the most popular openings in the 19th century's Romantic era of chess.
A fortress is a drawing technique where the defending side creates an impenetrable position despite being down material.
The Candidates Tournament format has changed many times throughout chess history, from match play to round-robin to knockout.
Mikhail Tal won the World Chess Championship title by defeating Mikhail Botvinnik in 1960, at the age of 23.
The Tarrasch Defense (1.d4 d5 2.c4 e6 3.Nc3 c5) leads to an isolated queen's pawn structure for Black.
Chess Olympiad is a biennial team event where countries send their best players to compete for gold, silver, and bronze medals.
The minority attack is a strategic plan where the side with fewer pawns on one wing advances them to create weaknesses.
Sven Magnus Øen Carlsen started playing chess at age 5 and became a grandmaster at age 13.
The Réti Opening (1.Nf3) is a hypermodern opening that delays committing the center pawns.
The Three-Move Checkmate (Scholar's Mate) is one of the first traps beginners learn: 1.e4 e5 2.Qh5 Nc6 3.Bc4 Nf6 4.Qxf7#.
International chess uses the algebraic notation system, where each square has a unique coordinate (a1 through h8).
The Catalan Opening combines elements of the Queen's Gambit with a king's bishop fianchetto (1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 e6 3.g3).
The Touch-Move rule states that if a player touches a piece, they must move it — if they touch an opponent's piece, they must capture it.
The World Chess Championship has been contested since 1886, making it one of the oldest championships in any sport.
A decoy is a tactic that lures an enemy piece to a disadvantageous square.
Fabiano Caruana became the first American to challenge for the World Chess Championship since Bobby Fischer when he faced Carlsen in 2018.
The Queen's Indian Defense (1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 e6 3.Nf3 b6) aims to control the center with pieces rather than pawns.
Bobby Fischer won the US Chess Championship with a perfect 11-0 score in 1963-64, one of the greatest tournament results ever.
The Sicilian Dragon gets its name because the pawn structure (pawns on d6, e7, f7, g6, h7) resembles the Draco constellation.
Kasparov accused Deep Blue's team of cheating after his 1997 loss, claiming the computer received human assistance.
The Philidor position and Lucena position are the two most important theoretical positions in rook endgames.
The Vienna Game (1.e4 e5 2.Nc3) is an opening that delays Nf3 to maintain the option of f2-f4.
Over 600 million people worldwide know how to play chess, making it one of the most widely played games.
World Chess Day is celebrated annually on July 20th, marking the founding date of FIDE in 1924.
A combination in chess is a sequence of forced moves that leads to a tangible advantage.
The 1972 Fischer-Spassky match in Reykjavik was nicknamed 'The Match of the Century.'
The Trompowsky Attack (1.d4 Nf6 2.Bg5) is a popular alternative to the more traditional Queen's Gambit lines.
Endgame tablebases have solved all positions with up to 7 pieces on the board, providing perfect play information.
The concept of 'space advantage' in chess refers to controlling more squares on the board than your opponent.
Alexander Grischuk is known for his severe time trouble, often playing critical moves with just seconds on his clock.
The Najdorf variation of the Sicilian Defense was a favorite of both Bobby Fischer and Garry Kasparov.
The concept of a 'positional sacrifice' involves giving up material for long-term strategic advantages rather than a direct attack.
Vishwanathan Anand held the World Chess Championship title from 2007 to 2013, successfully defending it twice.
Fool's Mate (1.f3 e5 2.g4 Qh4#) is the shortest possible checkmate, occurring in just two moves.
The Benko Gambit (1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 c5 3.d5 b5) involves a pawn sacrifice for long-term queenside pressure.
The chess clock was invented by Thomas Wilson in 1883, revolutionizing competitive chess.
An X-ray or skewer attack is when a piece attacks through another piece on the same line.
The term 'grandmaster' was first used informally in the early 20th century and became an official FIDE title in 1950.
Capablanca went undefeated for 8 years from 1916 to 1924, winning or drawing every serious game he played.
The Accelerated Dragon variation of the Sicilian avoids the Yugoslav Attack by delaying ...d6.
A windmill is a repeated discovered attack tactic, often involving a rook and bishop, that systematically captures material.
FIDE Master (FM) is a title awarded to players who achieve a rating of 2300 or higher.
The Colle System (1.d4 d5 2.Nf3 Nf6 3.e3) is a solid but unambitious opening system popular at club level.
Kasparov founded the Kasparov Chess Foundation to promote chess education in schools around the world.
The shortest decisive game in World Championship history lasted just 24 moves: Anand vs. Gelfand, Game 8, in 2012.
Triangulation is an endgame maneuver where the king takes three moves to reach a square it could reach in one, to lose a tempo.
The Chigorin Defense (1.d4 d5 2.c4 Nc6) is an unconventional opening that develops the knight before establishing a pawn chain.
Bobby Fischer forfeited Game 2 of his 1972 match against Spassky by refusing to play due to camera complaints.
The concept of prophylaxis in chess — preventing your opponent's plans before they execute them — was developed by Aron Nimzowitsch.
The Maróczy Bind is a pawn structure (pawns on c4 and e4) that restricts Black's counterplay in many Sicilian variations.
There are more possible chess games than atoms in the observable universe — the Shannon number estimates 10^120 possible games.
The Latvian Gambit (1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 f5) is one of the most aggressive and risky openings in chess.
Kramnik defeated Kasparov in 2000 to become the 14th World Champion, using the Berlin Defense to neutralize Kasparov's e4.
The concept of 'development' in chess refers to moving pieces from their starting positions to more active squares.
The Olympiad is the largest chess team event, with over 180 countries participating.
A clearance sacrifice removes one's own piece from a square to allow another piece to use that square.
Gata Kamsky was a child chess prodigy who defected from the Soviet Union and became a top American grandmaster.
The Two Knights Defense (1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.Bc4 Nf6) is a sharp alternative to the more solid Giuoco Piano.
Chess is one of the few sports where men and women can compete against each other on an equal playing field.
The concept of the 'bishop pair advantage' refers to the two bishops working together being worth more than bishop and knight or two knights.
The Candidates Tournament in 2022 was held in Madrid, Spain, with Ian Nepomniachtchi winning convincingly.
A Greek Gift sacrifice (Bxh7+) is a classic attacking pattern where a bishop is sacrificed on h7 to expose the opponent's king.
The Grob Attack (1.g4) is one of the most unusual and rarely played first moves in chess.
Magnus Carlsen has won the World Chess Championship five times, defending his title successfully in 2014, 2016, 2018, and 2021.
The Venice System (or Reversed Sicilian) is when White plays 1.c4 e5, essentially playing the Sicilian with an extra tempo.
An overloaded piece is one that has too many defensive responsibilities and cannot fulfill all of them simultaneously.
Hou Yifan, the youngest female World Chess Champion in history, won her first title at age 16.
The Semi-Tarrasch Defense (1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 e6 3.Nc3 d5 4.cxd5 exd5 5.Nf3 c5) is considered slightly more reliable than the Tarrasch.
The term 'en prise' means a piece is left undefended and can be captured for free.
The 1927 World Chess Championship between Capablanca and Alekhine lasted 34 games.
Chess pieces were originally carved from ivory, bone, and wood before plastic became the standard material.
A hanging pawn pair (two adjacent pawns without pawn support) can be either a strength or a weakness depending on the position.
The Hippopotamus Defense is an unusual setup where all pieces are developed behind the third rank.
Aron Nimzowitsch's book 'My System' (1925) is considered one of the most influential chess books ever written.
The smothered mate pattern occurs when a knight delivers checkmate to a king surrounded by its own pieces.
Magnus Carlsen won the World Blitz Championship a record-setting number of times.
The Ponziani Opening (1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.c3) is one of the oldest known openings but is rarely seen at top level.
Kasparov's 'My Great Predecessors' series is a monumental five-volume work analyzing the games of all previous World Champions.
A desperado is a piece that is about to be captured and tries to inflict maximum damage before it is taken.
The Nimzowitsch-Larsen Attack (1.b3) aims to fianchetto the queen's bishop and exert pressure on the long diagonal.
Levon Aronian is one of the strongest players from Armenia, a country where chess is taught as a mandatory school subject.
The concept of 'the initiative' in chess refers to the ability to make threats and force the opponent to react.
Boris Spassky was born in Leningrad (now St. Petersburg), Soviet Union.
The Dragon Sicilian's Yugoslav Attack (6.Be3) is one of the most aggressive systems White can employ.
An interference is a tactic where a piece is placed between an attacking piece and its target, disrupting the attack.
The grandmaster draw — a short, uneventful draw between top players — has been a controversial topic in chess for decades.
Vera Menchik was the first Women's World Chess Champion, holding the title from 1927 until her death in 1944.
The King's Indian Attack is a flexible system for White that can be used against many different Black setups.
The concept of 'weak squares' refers to squares that cannot be controlled or defended by pawns.
The Taimanov variation of the Sicilian (1.e4 c5 2.Nf3 e6 3.d4 cxd4 4.Nxd4 Nc6) offers Black flexible piece development.
Bobby Fischer disappeared from public life after winning the World Championship in 1972, not playing a competitive game for 20 years.
The first computer program to play chess was written by Alan Turing in 1950, though no computer was powerful enough to run it at the time.
A deflection is a tactic that forces a defending piece away from its critical square, leaving something unprotected.
The Open Catalan (1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 e6 3.g3 d5 4.Bg2 dxc4) allows Black to capture the c4 pawn, leading to asymmetrical play.
Max Euwe was the only Dutch player to become World Chess Champion, defeating Alekhine in 1935.
The Rauzer Attack in the Sicilian (6.Bg5 in the Classical Sicilian) leads to sharp, tactical play.
The 'Fischer boom' of the 1970s saw chess popularity skyrocket in the United States after Bobby Fischer's World Championship victory.
Anatoly Karpov is estimated to have won more chess tournaments than any other player in history — over 160.
The concept of 'color complex' refers to strategic patterns based on controlling squares of a particular color.
Domination is a tactic where a piece is trapped and cannot escape capture, even though it may have multiple squares available.
The Torre Attack (1.d4 Nf6 2.Nf3 e6 3.Bg5) is named after the Mexican grandmaster Carlos Torre Repetto.
Wijk aan Zee, a small Dutch village, transforms every January into the center of the chess world during the Tata Steel tournament.
The Botvinnik Semi-Slav (1.d4 d5 2.c4 c6 3.Nf3 Nf6 4.Nc3 e6 5.Bg5 dxc4 6.e4) is one of the most complex and theory-heavy openings in chess.
The 'opposition of colors' between bishop and pawns is crucial in many endgame positions — the bishop must be able to control the promotion square.
The first chess book ever printed was 'Repetición de Amores y Arte de Ajedrez' by Luis Ramírez de Lucena in 1497.
Anchor pawns are pawns that support a piece's outpost, keeping it secure on a strong square.
The Budapest Gambit (1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 e5) is a surprise weapon where Black sacrifices a pawn for active piece play.
The Internet Chess Club (ICC) was one of the first major online chess platforms, founded in 1995.
A fortress draw with bishop and wrong-colored rook pawn occurs when the defending king reaches the corner opposite to the bishop's color.
The concept of 'pawn islands' — groups of connected pawns separated by open files — is a way to assess pawn structure quality.
Teimour Radjabov became one of the world's top players while representing Azerbaijan.
Mikhail Chigorin was a pioneer of Russian chess in the late 19th century and helped establish Russia's chess tradition.
The first online chess game was reportedly played in 1987 over a network connecting users across the United States.
Nodirbek Abdusattorov won the World Rapid Championship in 2021 at just 17 years old, defeating Magnus Carlsen along the way.
The Bongcloud Attack (1.e4 e5 2.Ke2) became a famous meme opening when Magnus Carlsen played it in a serious online game.
The concept of 'coordination' in chess refers to how well pieces work together to attack or defend.
Fischer published his famous book 'My 60 Memorable Games' in 1969, which remains a classic of chess literature.
The Sicilian Kalashnikov (1.e4 c5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.d4 cxd4 4.Nxd4 e5 5.Nb5 d6) is related to the Sveshnikov but avoids certain main lines.
Bobby Fischer died in Reykjavik, Iceland, the same city where he won the World Championship 36 years earlier.
The seven pieces in chess are the king, queen, rook, bishop, knight, and pawn — each with unique movement rules.
A standard chess board has 64 squares arranged in an 8×8 grid of alternating light and dark colors.
The Breyer variation of the Ruy López (where Black plays ...Nb8) is a deep positional system favored by many top players.
Grandmaster Levon Aronian and other Armenian players have helped Armenia win three Olympiad gold medals.
A backward pawn is one that cannot be advanced because the adjacent pawns have already moved past it.
The concept of 'fortress breaking' involves finding ways to penetrate seemingly impregnable defensive setups.
The Najdorf Poisoned Pawn variation (6.Bg5 e6 7.f4 Qb6) is one of the sharpest lines in all of chess theory.
Samuel Reshevsky was a child chess prodigy who gave simultaneous exhibitions at age 8 in the 1920s.
The rule of the square is a quick way to determine if a king can catch a passed pawn in a pawn endgame.
The 2023 World Chess Championship match between Ding Liren and Ian Nepomniachtchi was decided in rapid tiebreaks.
Underpromotion — promoting a pawn to a knight, bishop, or rook instead of a queen — is rarely correct but occasionally essential.
The English Attack in the Sicilian Najdorf (6.Be3 followed by f3, Qd2, and g4) is a modern aggressive system.
Emanuel Lasker was not only a chess champion but also a mathematician and philosopher who wrote about game theory.
The passed pawn is often called 'the criminal who must be arrested' — a phrase attributed to Aron Nimzowitsch.

Caruana, Nakamura, and So made the United States one of the strongest chess nations in the 2010s.

A centralized knight on e5 or d5 is often considered as strong as a rook, as it controls many important squares.
Garri Kasparov received his chess education at the Mikhail Botvinnik chess school in the Soviet Union.
The Three-Check variant is a popular chess variant where the first player to deliver three checks wins.
